# Path-based allowlist for `validate_pool.py` privacy scan.
# One pattern per line. A file is exempted if its path (with both / and \ separators)
# contains any non-comment line below as a substring. Lines starting with `#` and
# blank lines are ignored.
#
# Use this only for files that legitimately contain example credentials, hashes,
# or terms in documentation/teaching contexts. Real leaks must be removed,
# never allowlisted.

# --- Legitimate documentation / teaching examples ---
# Paths are written without a leading `.github/` so they match both the
# canonical pool layout and the runtime-exported `.codex/` mirror.
instructions/security.instructions.md
instructions/mssql-dba.instructions.md
instructions/github-actions.instructions.md
agents/security-analyst.agent.md
skills/cloud/aws-agentic-ai/cross-service/credential-management.md
skills/cloud/aws-serverless-eda/references/security-best-practices.md
skills/cloud/building-mcp-server-on-cloudflare/references/oauth-setup.md
skills/devops/ci-cd-pipeline/SKILL.md
skills/frontend/brand-design/SKILL.md
skills/productivity/jira-issues/SKILL.md
skills/testing/playwright/lib/helpers.js

# --- Pool documentation referencing itself ---
agent-docs/agent-workflow-design-reference.md
agent-docs/PROJECT-ONBOARDING-RUNBOOK.md
agent-docs/RECIPE-RUNBOOK.md
recipes/enterprise-dashboard.recipe.md

# --- Remediation plan deliberately enumerates leaked tokens for cleanup tracking ---
plans/2026-04-27-ai-resource-pool-remediation.md
